title: The Docket - Week 8
subject: LexisNexis just made their biggest bet ever. It's not what you think.
preheader: Plus: the AI security checklist your IT team needs, and a tool that writes demand letters
bodyHtml:
The Docket

LegalTech AI Hub · Week of May 27, 2026

LexisNexis went shopping. And the thing they bought tells you exactly where legal AI is headed. Here’s your week.

🎯 LexisNexis Acquires AI Workflow Platform for $780M

LexisNexis didn’t buy another research tool. They bought FlowState, a workflow automation platform that connects AI tools into end-to-end legal processes. The signal is clear: the future isn’t individual AI tools, it’s AI-orchestrated workflows where research, drafting, review, and filing happen in a connected pipeline. Point solutions are becoming features. Platforms are becoming the product.

🛡️ First AI Security Standard for Law Firms Published

The International Legal Technology Association (ILTA) published the first comprehensive AI security standard for law firms. It covers data handling, prompt injection prevention, output validation, and vendor assessment. Every firm using AI tools should read this. It’s the closest thing we have to an industry standard, and it’s free. We’ve summarized the key requirements in our guides section.

💱 Legal AI Pricing Race to the Bottom

Three contract review platforms cut prices by 40-60% this month. The trigger: open-source models are now good enough for basic contract analysis, making it hard to charge premium prices for API-wrapper products. Winners: buyers. Losers: vendors who differentiated on “we use GPT-4” instead of building proprietary value. The commoditization of basic legal AI is happening faster than anyone expected.

Tool Spotlight

Legal AI Readiness Quiz

With all the changes happening this month, now’s a good time to reassess your firm’s AI readiness. Our quiz takes 5 minutes and gives you a customized score across five dimensions, plus specific recommendations for your firm size and practice area.

Big moves from the incumbents this week. The platform wars are officially underway. See you next Wednesday.
